intent: A privacy-centric banking platform that utilizes zero-knowledge proofs (ZKP) to anonymize transaction metadata on the public ledger while maintaining a dual-layer identity vault for regulatory compliance. The core workflow involves users submitting encrypted transaction requests that are verified mathematically without revealing sender/recipient details to the network, while the system retains a separate, strictly permissioned database linking these anonymous wallet addresses to verified legal identities for KYC/AML auditing.

entities.name: IdentityVault
entities.description: An air-gapped, HSM-backed repository that maps pseudonymous wallet addresses to PII, restricted to authorized compliance officers.

entities.name: ZKPTransactionEngine
entities.description: A high-performance cryptographic layer that processes input proofs to validate balance integrity and double-spend prevention without decrypting transaction payloads.

entities.name: ComplianceAuditorModule
entities.description: An automated oversight service that utilizes zero-knowledge reveal tokens to confirm transactions meet AML thresholds without exposing specific counterparty data to the public record.

entities.name: EphemeralWalletProxy
entities.description: A dynamic gateway that generates single-use public ledger addresses for each transaction to ensure metadata unlinkability.

entities.name: ProofVerificationLedger
entities.description: A permissioned blockchain hosting the immutable record of valid mathematical proofs, devoid of PII or clear-text financial metadata.

entities.name: KeyManagementService
entities.description: A distributed HSM cluster responsible for signing the bridging tokens that link the IdentityVault state to the EphemeralWalletProxy requests.

entities.name: RegulatoryAnchor
entities.description: A secure interface that stores cryptographic commitments (hashes) corresponding to legal identity files, enabling verifiable audits by regulators.

architecture.description: The system architecture initiates when a user triggers a transaction via the EphemeralWalletProxy, which requests a cryptographic signing token from the KeyManagementService. The ZKPTransactionEngine generates a proof of solvency and legitimacy, which is then submitted to the ProofVerificationLedger for immutable entry. Simultaneously, the IdentityVault records the transaction metadata in an encrypted, permissioned schema, bridging the link between the ephemeral address and the legal entity via a unique hash anchor. The ComplianceAuditorModule continuously polls the ProofVerificationLedger and matches these against the RegulatoryAnchor to ensure all transaction flows remain within KYC/AML compliance without ever exposing clear-text data to the public ledger nodes.

endpoints.summary: Generate a single-use public ledger address
Request a cryptographic signing token from the HSM
Process ZKP for balance integrity and double-spend
Securely link wallet address to PII
Retrieve proof status from the permissioned blockchain
Verify compliance status using reveal tokens
Store cryptographic commitment for audit trail

rules.description: Ensure double-spend prevention through ZKP verification.
rules.logic: The ProofVerificationLedger must reject any transaction proof if the unique nullifier associated with the input commitment has already been recorded in the ledger state.

rules.description: Enforce KYC/AML compliance via RegulatoryAnchor verification.
rules.logic: ComplianceAuditorModule must trigger an automatic transaction freeze if the aggregate volume associated with a RegulatoryAnchor commitment exceeds defined jurisdictional AML thresholds within a rolling 24-hour window.
The EphemeralWalletProxy must invalidate the public ledger address immediately upon successful confirmation of the ProofVerificationLedger entry, preventing address reuse.

The IdentityVault must deny any read request unless the requesting actor presents a valid, time-bound cryptographic grant signed by the KeyManagementService and matches a pre-approved compliance officer role.

rules.description: Maintain integrity between identity and transaction data.
rules.logic: The hash anchor bridging the IdentityVault and the transaction metadata must be cryptographically verified against the RegulatoryAnchor commitment before the record is persisted.
